source: NvChad - Install NvChad: the starter clone, first-run modules, lazy loading and the bootstrap

questions.q: How is the NvChad configuration installed?
options: By cloning the starter repository into ~/.config/nvim with git

questions.q: In `git clone ... starter ~/.config/nvim && nvim`, what does the `&& nvim` do?
options: Launches nvim, which initializes the just-downloaded configuration
questions.why: The clone downloads the starter; nvim then bootstraps it.

questions.q: On first start, which four modules are loaded (marked with a checkmark)?
questions.why: Only those four load initially; the rest are lazy-loaded.

questions.q: Which of those loaded modules provides the editor themes?
questions.why: base46 provides the themes; ui is the interface; treesitter does highlighting.

options: on demand, via lazy loading, when their functionality is requested
questions.why: Lazy loading improves startup time by deferring modules.

questions.q: In the bootstrap init.lua, the leader key (vim.g.mapleader) is set to...
Which plugin manager does the bootstrap install and then use?
questions.why: The bootstrap clones folke/lazy.nvim and runs require('lazy').setup.

questions.q: After installing plugins/parsers, which key closes the plugin manager screen?
questions.why: Press q to close it; :q quits the editor.
